detail.description:
Non-content metadata for a `notebook` object (§6.5 surfaces / §B): the per-space `slug` that is
the notebook's stable identity (`UNIQUE(space_id, slug)` in SQL) — the non-date analogue of
`JournalDayDetail.date`. The human title rides on `CanonicalObject.title` (tri-state, patchable);
the slug is identity (derived from the title at create, normalized at the FFI boundary), not a
content edit. The flow is the notebook's `content_units`, authored directly with §B `Heading` sections.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

units.description:
Scaffold units pre-created + persisted in the SAME transaction as the notebook — today the notation
home (one empty `config` block, family `notation`, at position 0). The glue inserts these on a
winning create.

items.description:
A content unit — the §6.0b `content_units` row. Content is the authored material;
`content_kind` is a DERIVED SQL column (the `content` tag), so it is NOT a field here.

id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

parent_unit_id.description: THE nesting mechanism: a child names its parent (must be in the same object, §6.1a).

slot.description:
Container-internal part where needed, e.g. `assumption` (case child) /
`justification` (proof_step child); `None` otherwise.

row_relation.description:
The relation this row asserts toward its prior sibling — a `Derivation` step's connective or
an `Equations` row's leading relation; `None` otherwise (the common `Equations` case).

type.description: THE one user-facing math-flow label; `None` = plain content.

status.description:
Per-unit crystallization state (§6.0/§2.4) — distinct from `MathExpression.parse_status`
(surface renderability) and from `ObjectStatus` (object lifecycle).

declared_by.description:
Who declared a unit's STRUCTURE (its type/kind) — a separate fact from content
provenance (§6.0). `ai` is deliberately absent: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` with AI provenance/derivation (§3.9).

extracted_structure.description: Optional candidate decomposition — declared, never written in slice 1 (§6.0).

kind.description: A value from the small named registry (e.g. `hypothesis_conclusion_decomposition`).

accepted_into.description: The version it materialized into, once accepted (audit trail) — `None` until then.

content.description:
A SINGLE unit's own authored material (§6.0) — never an embedded unit tree (children
are rows: `parent_unit_id` + `position` + `slot`). The internal `kind` tag is the
AUTHORITATIVE discriminator and the source of the derived `content_kind` SQL column.

oneOf.description: Prose + inline formatting + inline math + mentions.

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

oneOf.description: A formatting region over prose `text` — `span` may be non-empty (the marked range).

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

oneOf.description: Inline math — a zero-width atom (`span = [p, p]`); content lives in `expr`.

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

expr.description:
The one structure for all math, wherever it sits (§6.3a). Identity is STABLE and
presentation-independent; display mode is DERIVED from placement, so there is no mode
field. The `mathmeander` parser/serializer that fills these fields lands in slice 1b;
this is the wire shape it produces.

id.description:
Stable identity, minted in content (UUIDv7), unique workspace-wide — the
copy-mints-fresh / transclude-references rule hangs off this (§6.3a).

surface_text.description: The canonical surface in our grammar (names-canonical `mathmeander`).

original_input.description: Raw keystrokes, preserved verbatim — NEVER overwritten by normalization (§2.2).

parse_status.description:
Whether a surface is usable, per OUR parser (arch doc §6.3a) — defined by `mathmeander`,
not LaTeX. Un-parseable input is never punished: it persists as `invalid` with
`original_input` intact (§2.2).

- `renderable`    — parses cleanly into a fully-formed AST.
- `partially_resolved` — parses, but with one or more recovered error fragments
  (`Expr::Error`); still renderable, with the bad parts shown verbatim.
- `invalid`       — nothing usable parsed (e.g. empty after trimming, or all error).
- `unresolved`    — reserved for "parsed, but has unresolved occurrences/references"
  (set by the core once occurrence resolution runs; the parser never emits it).

occurrences.description:
Semantic refs inside the surface; each extracts to an edge (§6.1b). Always present
(`[]` when none) so the shape is explicit on both sides of the FFI.

items.description:
A semantic reference INSIDE a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). MVP selector is a coarse char
span (Q3); sub-term `Symbol` resolution (with `BoundVar`) is reserved. `target` is
absent while unresolved (the queue carries the unresolved text on the `links` row).

selector.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description: A mention — a zero-width atom (`span = [p, p]`); the surface lives in `text`.

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

text.description:
The literal reference surface ("Bolzano–Weierstrass") — kept verbatim; the
resolved edge lives in `links` (§6.1b).

anyOf.description:
Resolution target of a prose `Reference` marker (§6.0): a whole `Object`, or a specific `Unit`
(block) within one. `notation`/`source` arms are reserved (slices 2/3). Absent while unresolved.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description:
A block within an object — the citation grain for "the definition of an open set" /
"Theorem 3.2". `unit_id` is globally unique (so it survives any future re-homing); `object_id`
is its current home (a resolution hint and the derived Link's `target_object_id`).

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

link_id.description:
Identity of the `from_content` `Link` this mention derives — CLIENT-minted (like unit
ids; the core mints none), so it is STABLE across content edits and the edge is
reconciled by id, not by position. `None` = not yet stamped (no edge derived).
`save_content` derives/reconciles the edge from this and the `target`.

target_handle_id.description:
Which AUTHORED NAME this mention chose (§6.3b) — the `Handle` whose CURRENT string the
citation displays (rename → the citation updates; identity-not-characters, §3). `None` =
cite by number / the unit's primary name. Display-only: the derived `Link` still targets
the unit, so `save_content` ignores this field.

oneOf.description: A display/block PLACEMENT of one `MathExpression` — not a separate math model.

presentation-independent; display mode is DERIVED from placement, so there is no mode
field. The `mathmeander` parser/serializer that fills these fields lands in slice 1b;
this is the wire shape it produces.

copy-mints-fresh / transclude-references rule hangs off this (§6.3a).

A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description: Items are child rows.
Steps are child rows; each step child carries its relation (slice 1b+).

Co-equal, jointly-asserted relation rows — the "system of equations" `{2x+y=1; x−y=4}`.
Children are `Math`/`Prose` rows (`parent_unit_id` → this container); each row may carry a
`row_relation`. Names the MATHEMATICS, not the layout — column alignment is DERIVED at render
(never stored). Distinct from `Derivation` (sequential) and `CaseSplit` (conditional).
Each case is a child group; `slot` marks the assumption child vs body children.
THE generic container — a typed unit's multi-part body, or a neutral grouping.

A titled section anchor (§B). The title IS this unit's own prose — `text` + `inline`, with the
full prose vocabulary (formatting `Mark`s, inline `Math` atoms, `Reference` mentions), so a
heading can read "§3 Properties of $L^2$". The section's BODY and SUBSECTIONS are this unit's
CHILD rows (`parent_unit_id` → this heading); a subsection is just a child `Heading`. Because the
title lives ON the unit (not in a child), every section unit is 1:1 with one editor block — the
flat-flush invariant — and a section move is a single `reparent_unit` (the subtree follows). LEVEL
is nesting depth (no level field, §B). Distinct from the anonymous `Group`: a `Heading` is titled
and participates in the outline / numbering. The title edits freely through `save_content` (like
`Prose`); becoming/un-becoming a heading is the `toggle_heading` op (a content-KIND change, frozen
from the coarse delta like every other kind transition).

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

oneOf.description: A formatting region over prose `text` — `span` may be non-empty (the marked range).

shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

id.description:
Stable identity, minted in content (UUIDv7), unique workspace-wide — the
copy-mints-fresh / transclude-references rule hangs off this (§6.3a).

surface_text.description: The canonical surface in our grammar (names-canonical `mathmeander`).

original_input.description: Raw keystrokes, preserved verbatim — NEVER overwritten by normalization (§2.2).

span (Q3); sub-term `Symbol` resolution (with `BoundVar`) is reserved. `target` is

text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the

text.description:
The literal reference surface ("Bolzano–Weierstrass") — kept verbatim; the
resolved edge lives in `links` (§6.1b).

anyOf.description:
Resolution target of a prose `Reference` marker (§6.0): a whole `Object`, or a specific `Unit`
(block) within one. `notation`/`source` arms are reserved (slices 2/3). Absent while unresolved.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description:
A block within an object — the citation grain for "the definition of an open set" /
"Theorem 3.2". `unit_id` is globally unique (so it survives any future re-homing); `object_id`
is its current home (a resolution hint and the derived Link's `target_object_id`).

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

link_id.description:
Identity of the `from_content` `Link` this mention derives — CLIENT-minted (like unit
ids; the core mints none), so it is STABLE across content edits and the edge is
reconciled by id, not by position. `None` = not yet stamped (no edge derived).
`save_content` derives/reconciles the edge from this and the `target`.

target_handle_id.description:
Which AUTHORED NAME this mention chose (§6.3b) — the `Handle` whose CURRENT string the
citation displays (rename → the citation updates; identity-not-characters, §3). `None` =
cite by number / the unit's primary name. Display-only: the derived `Link` still targets
the unit, so `save_content` ignores this field.

oneOf.description: Quotes & object embeds.

target.description:
What an `embed` unit transcludes (§6.0/§6.5). Slice 1 supports the OBJECT arm;
`source_excerpt` is reserved until sources land (slice 3).

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description:
A config-family block (the notation home, §Design-model): declarative `source` interpreted by
`family`. The `source` is the canonical, lossless truth; any rendering/resolution is DERIVED
(notation-as-register — the literal source is never rewritten). A leaf (never a `parent_unit_id`);
its `source` edits freely through `save_content` like `Prose`, but a kind flip into/out of `Config`
is rejected like every other content-kind transition.

family.description:
The declarative category of a `UnitContent::Config` block — it selects the interpreter that reads the
block's `source` (notation → a render-time register; conventions / annotation-recipes / styles join
later as added variants, reusing the one block construct). Wire vocabulary is the variant name
(`"notation"`). A new family is an ADDITIVE enum variant + its own interpreter — the block / editor /
projection machinery is shared, the cascade is a property of config-family blocks.

oneOf.description: User-defined notation: `TRIGGER := EXPANSION` lines, resolved at render (notation-as-register).

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

CreatedObject.title: CreatedObject
CreatedObject.description: What a successful create yields: both rows the glue persists in ONE transaction.

object.description:
A canonical object — the doc's §6 `objects` row shape, verbatim (all eleven fields).
Content (`content_units`/MathContent) is a SEPARATE aggregate arriving in slice 1;
an object with zero units is valid, which is what makes that addition purely
structural. Named `CanonicalObject` (not `Object`) so the generated TS type never
shadows JavaScript's global `Object`.

id.description: Client-minted UUIDv7 (arch doc §4/§6.3).

type.description:
Durable workspace identity of an object (arch doc §6.0b: `object.type` ≠ `unit.type`).
Slice 1 widens this from the skeleton's lone `note` to the full §6 vocabulary: the
formal family (theorem/lemma/proposition/corollary/conjecture/claim), definition,
proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus `journal_day`, producible
since slice 2b via its §6.5 surface (not the plain typed POST). `source_excerpt`, `trail`,
and `annotation` remain **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet producible — their
detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

title.description: Tri-state (§6.3): `None` = unset, `Some("")` = explicitly empty — never collapsed.

raw_source.description: The rough input, preserved VERBATIM (§2.2). No normalization function exists.

status.description:
Object lifecycle (arch doc §5.2/§6) — the full, doc-stable set lands now even though
the skeleton's create path can only PRODUCE `Draft`; other values become producible
via future status-transition operations.

schema_version.description: Application-level model version (§6.3); migrated by the core's total functions.

revision.description: Optimistic-concurrency token (§6.4): increments on every persisted write.

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

provenance.description:
Provenance — the typed trust spine (arch doc §6.1). One row per provenanced fact;
origin-specific fields (model, prompt_template, context_snapshot_id, review_item_id,
source_id, source_locator) arrive as `Option` fields together with their target
tables — additive in Rust and SQL simultaneously.

id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

origin.description:
Provenance origin (arch doc §6.1). Stable, complete set; only `User` and `System`
are producible until the AI/import provenance columns land with their slices.

created_by.description:
User/agent id. Required when `origin = user` (arch doc §6.1a) — enforced by
validation, not by the shape.

DeclaredBy.title: DeclaredBy
DeclaredBy.description:
Who declared a unit's STRUCTURE (its type/kind) — a separate fact from content
provenance (§6.0). `ai` is deliberately absent: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` with AI provenance/derivation (§3.9).

DefinitionDetail.title: DefinitionDetail
DefinitionDetail.description:
Non-content metadata for a `definition` object (§6.1c): the definiendum, which is
object identity. The body is flowing units, not a column.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

DisplayLabels.title: DisplayLabels
DisplayLabels.description: The projection's output: one `UnitLabel` per input unit, in stable document order.

items.description:
One unit's computed label facets (§6.3b, decision G). BOTH are returned; presentation
decides whether the name supersedes the number. `number`/`name` are independently optional.

unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

unit_type.description:
Echoed so presentation can render "Theorem 3" without re-reading the unit. `None` for
a plain (typeless) unit.

number.description:
The 1-based ordinal among its counter class; `None` when the policy doesn't number this
unit's type.

name.description:
A user name override (a `handle` on the unit, or — for an object `embed` — the embedded
object's `alias`). Where present it is what a chip shows; the `number` is still returned.

DissolveObjectInput.title: DissolveObjectInput
DissolveObjectInput.description:
`dissolve_object` payload — the inverse of `rehome_subtree` (§9.y reversibility). Folds an
embedded object's content back into its host (ids preserved), removing the embed and the object.
`inbound_references` is the glue-loaded list of edges/handles depending on the object's identity
(the core counts nothing — it stays pure of the reference query, §6.1b); a NON-EMPTY list makes
dissolution a reviewable refusal (`DissolutionBlocked`), never a silent move (§9.y).

A destructive write carries TWO concurrency gates (unlike the creative `rehome_subtree`, which
mints a fresh object and needs only the host gate): `expected_revision` gates the HOST and
`expected_dissolved_revision` gates the object being DESTROYED, so a concurrent edit to either
between load and dissolve loses the race (409) rather than being silently dropped (§6.4). Both
ride for the glue's conditional writes; the core reads neither.

expected_dissolved_revision.description: The expected revision of the object being DESTROYED — the second gate (§6.4).

host_content.description:
The working content aggregate an op transforms: one object's units plus its concurrency
token. NOT a persisted row — the glue assembles it from `content_units` + `objects.revision`
(§6.0b/§6.4) and writes the resulting `OpOutcome` back.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

items.description:
A content unit — the §6.0b `content_units` row. Content is the authored material;
`content_kind` is a DERIVED SQL column (the `content` tag), so it is NOT a field here.

id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

parent_unit_id.description: THE nesting mechanism: a child names its parent (must be in the same object, §6.1a).

slot.description:
Container-internal part where needed, e.g. `assumption` (case child) /
`justification` (proof_step child); `None` otherwise.

row_relation.description:
The relation this row asserts toward its prior sibling — a `Derivation` step's connective or
an `Equations` row's leading relation; `None` otherwise (the common `Equations` case).

type.description: THE one user-facing math-flow label; `None` = plain content.

status.description:
Per-unit crystallization state (§6.0/§2.4) — distinct from `MathExpression.parse_status`
(surface renderability) and from `ObjectStatus` (object lifecycle).

declared_by.description:
Who declared a unit's STRUCTURE (its type/kind) — a separate fact from content
provenance (§6.0). `ai` is deliberately absent: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` with AI provenance/derivation (§3.9).

extracted_structure.description: Optional candidate decomposition — declared, never written in slice 1 (§6.0).

kind.description: A value from the small named registry (e.g. `hypothesis_conclusion_decomposition`).

accepted_into.description: The version it materialized into, once accepted (audit trail) — `None` until then.

content.description:
A SINGLE unit's own authored material (§6.0) — never an embedded unit tree (children
are rows: `parent_unit_id` + `position` + `slot`). The internal `kind` tag is the
AUTHORITATIVE discriminator and the source of the derived `content_kind` SQL column.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description: Items are child rows.
Steps are child rows; each step child carries its relation (slice 1b+).

Co-equal, jointly-asserted relation rows — the "system of equations" `{2x+y=1; x−y=4}`.
Children are `Math`/`Prose` rows (`parent_unit_id` → this container); each row may carry a
`row_relation`. Names the MATHEMATICS, not the layout — column alignment is DERIVED at render
(never stored). Distinct from `Derivation` (sequential) and `CaseSplit` (conditional).
Each case is a child group; `slot` marks the assumption child vs body children.
THE generic container — a typed unit's multi-part body, or a neutral grouping.

A titled section anchor (§B). The title IS this unit's own prose — `text` + `inline`, with the
full prose vocabulary (formatting `Mark`s, inline `Math` atoms, `Reference` mentions), so a
heading can read "§3 Properties of $L^2$". The section's BODY and SUBSECTIONS are this unit's
CHILD rows (`parent_unit_id` → this heading); a subsection is just a child `Heading`. Because the
title lives ON the unit (not in a child), every section unit is 1:1 with one editor block — the
flat-flush invariant — and a section move is a single `reparent_unit` (the subtree follows). LEVEL
is nesting depth (no level field, §B). Distinct from the anonymous `Group`: a `Heading` is titled
and participates in the outline / numbering. The title edits freely through `save_content` (like
`Prose`); becoming/un-becoming a heading is the `toggle_heading` op (a content-KIND change, frozen
from the coarse delta like every other kind transition).

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description:

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

link_id.description:
Identity of the `from_content` `Link` this mention derives — CLIENT-minted (like unit
ids; the core mints none), so it is STABLE across content edits and the edge is
reconciled by id, not by position. `None` = not yet stamped (no edge derived).
`save_content` derives/reconciles the edge from this and the `target`.

target_handle_id.description:
Which AUTHORED NAME this mention chose (§6.3b) — the `Handle` whose CURRENT string the
citation displays (rename → the citation updates; identity-not-characters, §3). `None` =
cite by number / the unit's primary name. Display-only: the derived `Link` still targets
the unit, so `save_content` ignores this field.

oneOf.description: Quotes & object embeds.

target.description:
What an `embed` unit transcludes (§6.0/§6.5). Slice 1 supports the OBJECT arm;
`source_excerpt` is reserved until sources land (slice 3).

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description:
A config-family block (the notation home, §Design-model): declarative `source` interpreted by
`family`. The `source` is the canonical, lossless truth; any rendering/resolution is DERIVED
(notation-as-register — the literal source is never rewritten). A leaf (never a `parent_unit_id`);
its `source` edits freely through `save_content` like `Prose`, but a kind flip into/out of `Config`
is rejected like every other content-kind transition.

family.description:
The declarative category of a `UnitContent::Config` block — it selects the interpreter that reads the
block's `source` (notation → a render-time register; conventions / annotation-recipes / styles join
later as added variants, reusing the one block construct). Wire vocabulary is the variant name
(`"notation"`). A new family is an ADDITIVE enum variant + its own interpreter — the block / editor /
projection machinery is shared, the cascade is a property of config-family blocks.

oneOf.description: User-defined notation: `TRIGGER := EXPANSION` lines, resolved at render (notation-as-register).

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

embed_unit_id.description: The `Embed{target: Object{dissolved_object_id}}` unit in the host to replace.

dissolved_object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

dissolved_content.description:
The working content aggregate an op transforms: one object's units plus its concurrency
token. NOT a persisted row — the glue assembles it from `content_units` + `objects.revision`
(§6.0b/§6.4) and writes the resulting `OpOutcome` back.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

items.description:
A content unit — the §6.0b `content_units` row. Content is the authored material;
`content_kind` is a DERIVED SQL column (the `content` tag), so it is NOT a field here.

id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

parent_unit_id.description: THE nesting mechanism: a child names its parent (must be in the same object, §6.1a).

slot.description:
Container-internal part where needed, e.g. `assumption` (case child) /
`justification` (proof_step child); `None` otherwise.

row_relation.description:
The relation this row asserts toward its prior sibling — a `Derivation` step's connective or
an `Equations` row's leading relation; `None` otherwise (the common `Equations` case).

type.description: THE one user-facing math-flow label; `None` = plain content.

status.description:
Per-unit crystallization state (§6.0/§2.4) — distinct from `MathExpression.parse_status`
(surface renderability) and from `ObjectStatus` (object lifecycle).

declared_by.description:
Who declared a unit's STRUCTURE (its type/kind) — a separate fact from content
provenance (§6.0). `ai` is deliberately absent: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` with AI provenance/derivation (§3.9).

extracted_structure.description: Optional candidate decomposition — declared, never written in slice 1 (§6.0).

kind.description: A value from the small named registry (e.g. `hypothesis_conclusion_decomposition`).

accepted_into.description: The version it materialized into, once accepted (audit trail) — `None` until then.

content.description:
A SINGLE unit's own authored material (§6.0) — never an embedded unit tree (children
are rows: `parent_unit_id` + `position` + `slot`). The internal `kind` tag is the
AUTHORITATIVE discriminator and the source of the derived `content_kind` SQL column.

oneOf.description: Prose + inline formatting + inline math + mentions.

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description: Items are child rows.
Steps are child rows; each step child carries its relation (slice 1b+).

Co-equal, jointly-asserted relation rows — the "system of equations" `{2x+y=1; x−y=4}`.
Children are `Math`/`Prose` rows (`parent_unit_id` → this container); each row may carry a
`row_relation`. Names the MATHEMATICS, not the layout — column alignment is DERIVED at render
(never stored). Distinct from `Derivation` (sequential) and `CaseSplit` (conditional).
Each case is a child group; `slot` marks the assumption child vs body children.
THE generic container — a typed unit's multi-part body, or a neutral grouping.

A titled section anchor (§B). The title IS this unit's own prose — `text` + `inline`, with the
full prose vocabulary (formatting `Mark`s, inline `Math` atoms, `Reference` mentions), so a
heading can read "§3 Properties of $L^2$". The section's BODY and SUBSECTIONS are this unit's
CHILD rows (`parent_unit_id` → this heading); a subsection is just a child `Heading`. Because the
title lives ON the unit (not in a child), every section unit is 1:1 with one editor block — the
flat-flush invariant — and a section move is a single `reparent_unit` (the subtree follows). LEVEL
is nesting depth (no level field, §B). Distinct from the anonymous `Group`: a `Heading` is titled
and participates in the outline / numbering. The title edits freely through `save_content` (like
`Prose`); becoming/un-becoming a heading is the `toggle_heading` op (a content-KIND change, frozen
from the coarse delta like every other kind transition).

EmbedTarget.description:
What an `embed` unit transcludes (§6.0/§6.5). Slice 1 supports the OBJECT arm;
`source_excerpt` is reserved until sources land (slice 3).

EquationRowInput.title: EquationRowInput
EquationRowInput.description:
One co-equal row of an `Equations` system (a `Math` line or a `Prose` annotation), with its
optional leading relation. `content` must be `Math`/`Prose` (else `EquationsRowNotPermitted`).

unit_id.description: Glue-minted id for this row unit.

content.description: `Math { expr }` (client-built) or `Prose { .. }`.

oneOf.description: Prose + inline formatting + inline math + mentions.

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

oneOf.description: A formatting region over prose `text` — `span` may be non-empty (the marked range).

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

oneOf.description: Inline math — a zero-width atom (`span = [p, p]`); content lives in `expr`.

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

expr.description:
The one structure for all math, wherever it sits (§6.3a). Identity is STABLE and
presentation-independent; display mode is DERIVED from placement, so there is no mode
field. The `mathmeander` parser/serializer that fills these fields lands in slice 1b;
this is the wire shape it produces.

id.description:
Stable identity, minted in content (UUIDv7), unique workspace-wide — the
copy-mints-fresh / transclude-references rule hangs off this (§6.3a).

surface_text.description: The canonical surface in our grammar (names-canonical `mathmeander`).

original_input.description: Raw keystrokes, preserved verbatim — NEVER overwritten by normalization (§2.2).

parse_status.description:
Whether a surface is usable, per OUR parser (arch doc §6.3a) — defined by `mathmeander`,
not LaTeX. Un-parseable input is never punished: it persists as `invalid` with
`original_input` intact (§2.2).

- `renderable`    — parses cleanly into a fully-formed AST.
- `partially_resolved` — parses, but with one or more recovered error fragments
  (`Expr::Error`); still renderable, with the bad parts shown verbatim.
- `invalid`       — nothing usable parsed (e.g. empty after trimming, or all error).
- `unresolved`    — reserved for "parsed, but has unresolved occurrences/references"
  (set by the core once occurrence resolution runs; the parser never emits it).

occurrences.description:
Semantic refs inside the surface; each extracts to an edge (§6.1b). Always present
(`[]` when none) so the shape is explicit on both sides of the FFI.

items.description:
A semantic reference INSIDE a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). MVP selector is a coarse char
span (Q3); sub-term `Symbol` resolution (with `BoundVar`) is reserved. `target` is
absent while unresolved (the queue carries the unresolved text on the `links` row).

selector.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description: A mention — a zero-width atom (`span = [p, p]`); the surface lives in `text`.

A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in

text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

text.description:
The literal reference surface ("Bolzano–Weierstrass") — kept verbatim; the
resolved edge lives in `links` (§6.1b).

anyOf.description:
Resolution target of a prose `Reference` marker (§6.0): a whole `Object`, or a specific `Unit`
(block) within one. `notation`/`source` arms are reserved (slices 2/3). Absent while unresolved.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description:
A block within an object — the citation grain for "the definition of an open set" /
"Theorem 3.2". `unit_id` is globally unique (so it survives any future re-homing); `object_id`
is its current home (a resolution hint and the derived Link's `target_object_id`).

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

link_id.description:
Identity of the `from_content` `Link` this mention derives — CLIENT-minted (like unit
ids; the core mints none), so it is STABLE across content edits and the edge is
reconciled by id, not by position. `None` = not yet stamped (no edge derived).
`save_content` derives/reconciles the edge from this and the `target`.

target_handle_id.description:
Which AUTHORED NAME this mention chose (§6.3b) — the `Handle` whose CURRENT string the
citation displays (rename → the citation updates; identity-not-characters, §3). `None` =
cite by number / the unit's primary name. Display-only: the derived `Link` still targets
the unit, so `save_content` ignores this field.

oneOf.description: A display/block PLACEMENT of one `MathExpression` — not a separate math model.

A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description: Items are child rows.
Steps are child rows; each step child carries its relation (slice 1b+).

Co-equal, jointly-asserted relation rows — the "system of equations" `{2x+y=1; x−y=4}`.
Children are `Math`/`Prose` rows (`parent_unit_id` → this container); each row may carry a
`row_relation`. Names the MATHEMATICS, not the layout — column alignment is DERIVED at render
(never stored). Distinct from `Derivation` (sequential) and `CaseSplit` (conditional).
Each case is a child group; `slot` marks the assumption child vs body children.
THE generic container — a typed unit's multi-part body, or a neutral grouping.

A titled section anchor (§B). The title IS this unit's own prose — `text` + `inline`, with the
full prose vocabulary (formatting `Mark`s, inline `Math` atoms, `Reference` mentions), so a
heading can read "§3 Properties of $L^2$". The section's BODY and SUBSECTIONS are this unit's
CHILD rows (`parent_unit_id` → this heading); a subsection is just a child `Heading`. Because the
title lives ON the unit (not in a child), every section unit is 1:1 with one editor block — the
flat-flush invariant — and a section move is a single `reparent_unit` (the subtree follows). LEVEL
is nesting depth (no level field, §B). Distinct from the anonymous `Group`: a `Heading` is titled
and participates in the outline / numbering. The title edits freely through `save_content` (like
`Prose`); becoming/un-becoming a heading is the `toggle_heading` op (a content-KIND change, frozen
from the coarse delta like every other kind transition).

oneOf.description: Quotes & object embeds.

target.description:
What an `embed` unit transcludes (§6.0/§6.5). Slice 1 supports the OBJECT arm;
`source_excerpt` is reserved until sources land (slice 3).

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description:
A config-family block (the notation home, §Design-model): declarative `source` interpreted by
`family`. The `source` is the canonical, lossless truth; any rendering/resolution is DERIVED
(notation-as-register — the literal source is never rewritten). A leaf (never a `parent_unit_id`);
its `source` edits freely through `save_content` like `Prose`, but a kind flip into/out of `Config`
is rejected like every other content-kind transition.

family.description:
The declarative category of a `UnitContent::Config` block — it selects the interpreter that reads the
block's `source` (notation → a render-time register; conventions / annotation-recipes / styles join
later as added variants, reusing the one block construct). Wire vocabulary is the variant name
(`"notation"`). A new family is an ADDITIVE enum variant + its own interpreter — the block / editor /
projection machinery is shared, the cascade is a property of config-family blocks.

oneOf.description: User-defined notation: `TRIGGER := EXPANSION` lines, resolved at render (notation-as-register).

row_relation.description: The relation this row asserts (alignment/connective); `None` if intrinsic to its content.

ExampleKind.title: ExampleKind
ExampleKind.description:
Optional refinement of `type = example` (§6.0). Never a second classifying act — cues
like "Non-example:" expand to type + kind in one gesture (§9.y), or it is set later.

ExpressionIdRemap.title: ExpressionIdRemap
ExpressionIdRemap.description: One old→new expression-id mapping (materialize copies mint fresh ids, §6.3a).

from.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

to.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

ExtractedStructureEnvelope.title: ExtractedStructureEnvelope
ExtractedStructureEnvelope.description:
The mandatory envelope every `extracted_structure` candidate carries (§6.0). This is a
CANDIDATE/working layer — never canonical knowledge — and slice 1 DECLARES it with NO
write or acceptance path (acceptance materializes into units/links/typed metadata in
slice 5). The version binding (`base_object_revision`) mirrors the review-candidate
discipline (§6.4) at unit scale.

kind.description: A value from the small named registry (e.g. `hypothesis_conclusion_decomposition`).

accepted_into.description: The version it materialized into, once accepted (audit trail) — `None` until then.

Handle.title: Handle
Handle.description:
A handle — an optional human name for an INTRA-object element (a unit or an
expression), §6.3b. Bound to the owning object (always) refined by exactly one of
unit / expression. Objects are named by `aliases`, not handles.

id.description:
Identity of a handle (`handles` row, §6.3b) — a user name for an intra-object
element (a unit or expression).

target_object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

target_expression_id.description:
A `MathExpression` id, resolved via the expression→unit index (§6.3a). Exactly one
of `{target_unit_id, target_expression_id}` is set (§6.1a).

status.description:
Handle lifecycle (§6.3b): a handle whose target is gone goes `stale` to review,
never silently (§6.1b).

scope.description: Uniqueness/conflict scope of a handle (§6.3b).

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

HandleStatus.title: HandleStatus
HandleStatus.description:
Handle lifecycle (§6.3b): a handle whose target is gone goes `stale` to review,
never silently (§6.1b).

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

InputSyntax.title: InputSyntax

InsertEquationsInput.title: InsertEquationsInput
InsertEquationsInput.description:
`insert_equations` payload — mint an `Equations` container + its co-equal `Math`/`Prose` child
rows in ONE op. This is the ONLY creation path for the container (the editor's `save_content`
delta rejects new non-prose units, §6.0a). The container is inserted as a sibling immediately
AFTER `anchor_unit_id`, inheriting its placement context (parent / status / declared_by /
provenance), mirroring `displayize`. Each row carries PRE-BUILT content — the client built every
`MathExpression` via the keystone `normalize_fresh` + a client-minted expr id; the core never
parses surfaces here (the before-anchors invariant, §6.3a). Unit-level ids (container + rows) are
glue-minted.

anchor_unit_id.description: The existing sibling to insert the container after; it supplies the placement context.

container_unit_id.description: Glue-minted id for the new container unit.

rows.description: The rows, in document order (each a co-equal line of the system).

items.description:
One co-equal row of an `Equations` system (a `Math` line or a `Prose` annotation), with its
optional leading relation. `content` must be `Math`/`Prose` (else `EquationsRowNotPermitted`).

unit_id.description: Glue-minted id for this row unit.

content.description: `Math { expr }` (client-built) or `Prose { .. }`.

oneOf.description: Prose + inline formatting + inline math + mentions.

items.description:

expr.description:
The one structure for all math, wherever it sits (§6.3a). Identity is STABLE and
presentation-independent; display mode is DERIVED from placement, so there is no mode
field. The `mathmeander` parser/serializer that fills these fields lands in slice 1b;
this is the wire shape it produces.

id.description:
Stable identity, minted in content (UUIDv7), unique workspace-wide — the
copy-mints-fresh / transclude-references rule hangs off this (§6.3a).

surface_text.description: The canonical surface in our grammar (names-canonical `mathmeander`).

original_input.description: Raw keystrokes, preserved verbatim — NEVER overwritten by normalization (§2.2).

parse_status.description:
Whether a surface is usable, per OUR parser (arch doc §6.3a) — defined by `mathmeander`,
not LaTeX. Un-parseable input is never punished: it persists as `invalid` with
`original_input` intact (§2.2).

- `renderable`    — parses cleanly into a fully-formed AST.
- `partially_resolved` — parses, but with one or more recovered error fragments
  (`Expr::Error`); still renderable, with the bad parts shown verbatim.
- `invalid`       — nothing usable parsed (e.g. empty after trimming, or all error).
- `unresolved`    — reserved for "parsed, but has unresolved occurrences/references"
  (set by the core once occurrence resolution runs; the parser never emits it).

occurrences.description:
Semantic refs inside the surface; each extracts to an edge (§6.1b). Always present
(`[]` when none) so the shape is explicit on both sides of the FFI.

items.description:
A semantic reference INSIDE a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). MVP selector is a coarse char
span (Q3); sub-term `Symbol` resolution (with `BoundVar`) is reserved. `target` is
absent while unresolved (the queue carries the unresolved text on the `links` row).

resolved edge lives in `links` (§6.1b).

anyOf.description:
Resolution target of a prose `Reference` marker (§6.0): a whole `Object`, or a specific `Unit`

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description:
A block within an object — the citation grain for "the definition of an open set" /
"Theorem 3.2". `unit_id` is globally unique (so it survives any future re-homing); `object_id`

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

link_id.description:
Identity of the `from_content` `Link` this mention derives — CLIENT-minted (like unit
ids; the core mints none), so it is STABLE across content edits and the edge is
reconciled by id, not by position. `None` = not yet stamped (no edge derived).
`save_content` derives/reconciles the edge from this and the `target`.

target_handle_id.description:
Which AUTHORED NAME this mention chose (§6.3b) — the `Handle` whose CURRENT string the
citation displays (rename → the citation updates; identity-not-characters, §3). `None` =
cite by number / the unit's primary name. Display-only: the derived `Link` still targets
the unit, so `save_content` ignores this field.

oneOf.description: A display/block PLACEMENT of one `MathExpression` — not a separate math model.

oneOf.description: Items are child rows.
Steps are child rows; each step child carries its relation (slice 1b+).

Co-equal, jointly-asserted relation rows — the "system of equations" `{2x+y=1; x−y=4}`.
Children are `Math`/`Prose` rows (`parent_unit_id` → this container); each row may carry a
`row_relation`. Names the MATHEMATICS, not the layout — column alignment is DERIVED at render
(never stored). Distinct from `Derivation` (sequential) and `CaseSplit` (conditional).
Each case is a child group; `slot` marks the assumption child vs body children.
THE generic container — a typed unit's multi-part body, or a neutral grouping.

A titled section anchor (§B). The title IS this unit's own prose — `text` + `inline`, with the
full prose vocabulary (formatting `Mark`s, inline `Math` atoms, `Reference` mentions), so a
heading can read "§3 Properties of $L^2$". The section's BODY and SUBSECTIONS are this unit's
CHILD rows (`parent_unit_id` → this heading); a subsection is just a child `Heading`. Because the
title lives ON the unit (not in a child), every section unit is 1:1 with one editor block — the
flat-flush invariant — and a section move is a single `reparent_unit` (the subtree follows). LEVEL
is nesting depth (no level field, §B). Distinct from the anonymous `Group`: a `Heading` is titled
and participates in the outline / numbering. The title edits freely through `save_content` (like
`Prose`); becoming/un-becoming a heading is the `toggle_heading` op (a content-KIND change, frozen
from the coarse delta like every other kind transition).

oneOf.description: Quotes & object embeds.

target.description:
What an `embed` unit transcludes (§6.0/§6.5). Slice 1 supports the OBJECT arm;
`source_excerpt` is reserved until sources land (slice 3).

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description:
A config-family block (the notation home, §Design-model): declarative `source` interpreted by
`family`. The `source` is the canonical, lossless truth; any rendering/resolution is DERIVED
(notation-as-register — the literal source is never rewritten). A leaf (never a `parent_unit_id`);
its `source` edits freely through `save_content` like `Prose`, but a kind flip into/out of `Config`
is rejected like every other content-kind transition.

family.description:
The declarative category of a `UnitContent::Config` block — it selects the interpreter that reads the
block's `source` (notation → a render-time register; conventions / annotation-recipes / styles join
later as added variants, reusing the one block construct). Wire vocabulary is the variant name
(`"notation"`). A new family is an ADDITIVE enum variant + its own interpreter — the block / editor /
projection machinery is shared, the cascade is a property of config-family blocks.

oneOf.description: User-defined notation: `TRIGGER := EXPANSION` lines, resolved at render (notation-as-register).

row_relation.description: The relation this row asserts (alignment/connective); `None` if intrinsic to its content.

link.description:
The client-suppliable fields of a `links` row (§6.1b). The op stamps `status`,
`provenance_id`, and `created_at`, then runs `validate_link` — so a draft can never set the
trust-spine fields or bypass the §6.1a invariants.

id.description: Identity of an edge (`links` row, §6.1b).

source_object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

target_object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

target_unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

anyOf.description:
A finer-than-unit refinement of an edge target (§6.1d). Unit-level refinement is the
FK column `target_unit_id` (never duplicated as a selector — one home). `StructuralPath`
(sub-term addressing) is reserved (§14 structural editing) and deliberately NOT declared
in slice 1.

expression_id.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

link_type.description:
Typed relationship of a `links` edge (§6.1b). Most are deliberate graph edges
(object-target, §6.1a); `related`/content-derived rows may stay unresolved.

oneOf.description:
A copied/derived object points back at its origin (§6.1b). Produced by the
`materialize_object` copy-and-edge stand-in (slice 1c); general enough to cover
future non-copy derivations (re-home vs origin-reference is slice 2, §18.5).

source_unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

anyOf.description:
Where in a unit's content an edge sits (§6.1d). Paired with the `source_unit_id`
column (the unit is context-supplied). The SQL `in_expression` column is DERIVED from
`kind = expression_span` — never written independently.

oneOf.description: A span within the unit's prose text.
A span within an expression's surface — stable across inline/display toggles (§6.3a).

expression_id.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

JournalDayDetail.title: JournalDayDetail
JournalDayDetail.description:
Non-content metadata for a `journal_day` object (§6.5 surfaces): the calendar date this
day's flow belongs to. A `journal_day` owns ONE day's `content_units`; the *journal* is the
view over these objects ORDER BY date, and an object's appearance on a day is an
`Embed{target: Object}` unit. The date is object identity (`UNIQUE(space_id, date)` in SQL),
not patchable content — re-dating is a deliberate §6.5 content-move op, never a column edit.
`NaiveDate` serde-round-trips as ISO `YYYY-MM-DD`; chrono is built clock-free (the date is
PASSED IN), so the core stays pure.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

Link.title: Link
Link.description:
An edge — the §6.1b `links` row. Slice 1 supports a TWO-ARM target (`target_object_id`
xor `unresolved_text`); the notation/source arms widen in slices 2/3 when their FK
targets exist. `target_unit_id`/`target_selector` are REFINEMENTS of the object target,
never target alternatives. `in_expression` is a DERIVED SQL column (not a field).

id.description: Identity of an edge (`links` row, §6.1b).

target_object_id.description: The object target (one of the two slice-1 target arms).

unresolved_text.description: The literal reference text while UNRESOLVED — the other slice-1 target arm.

target_selector.description: Finer-than-unit refinement of the object target (§6.1d).

in slice 1.

independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

LinkDraft.title: LinkDraft
LinkDraft.description:
The client-suppliable fields of a `links` row (§6.1b). The op stamps `status`,
`provenance_id`, and `created_at`, then runs `validate_link` — so a draft can never set the
trust-spine fields or bypass the §6.1a invariants.

id.description: Identity of an edge (`links` row, §6.1b).

source_object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

target_object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

target_unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

anyOf.description:
A finer-than-unit refinement of an edge target (§6.1d). Unit-level refinement is the
FK column `target_unit_id` (never duplicated as a selector — one home). `StructuralPath`
(sub-term addressing) is reserved (§14 structural editing) and deliberately NOT declared
in slice 1.

expression_id.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

link_type.description:
Typed relationship of a `links` edge (§6.1b). Most are deliberate graph edges
(object-target, §6.1a); `related`/content-derived rows may stay unresolved.

oneOf.description:
A copied/derived object points back at its origin (§6.1b). Produced by the
`materialize_object` copy-and-edge stand-in (slice 1c); general enough to cover
future non-copy derivations (re-home vs origin-reference is slice 2, §18.5).

source_unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

anyOf.description:
Where in a unit's content an edge sits (§6.1d). Paired with the `source_unit_id`
column (the unit is context-supplied). The SQL `in_expression` column is DERIVED from
`kind = expression_span` — never written independently.

oneOf.description: A span within the unit's prose text.
A span within an expression's surface — stable across inline/display toggles (§6.3a).

expression_id.description:
Identity of a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). Minted in content, presentation-
independent, and unique workspace-wide (the copy-mints-fresh rule, §6.3a).
Lives inside serialized `UnitContent`, not its own table.

LinkStatus.title: LinkStatus
LinkStatus.description:
Edge lifecycle (§6.1b). Marker-derived edges never go stale (they sync with or die
with their markers); canonical edges whose basis vanished go `stale` to review.

oneOf.description:
A copied/derived object points back at its origin (§6.1b). Produced by the
`materialize_object` copy-and-edge stand-in (slice 1c); general enough to cover
future non-copy derivations (re-home vs origin-reference is slice 2, §18.5).

LinkType.title: LinkType
LinkType.description:
Typed relationship of a `links` edge (§6.1b). Most are deliberate graph edges
(object-target, §6.1a); `related`/content-derived rows may stay unresolved.

MaterializeObjectInput.title: MaterializeObjectInput
MaterializeObjectInput.description:
`materialize_object` payload — the COPY path (deliberate duplication / paste-as-reference,
§18.7; not the greedy-capture materialize, which is `rehome_subtree`). The glue pre-mints the new
object/provenance/edge ids and TOTAL id remaps over `source_content` (every unit and every
expression must have an entry, else `RemapIncomplete` — a partial map would alias ids across
objects). Re-mints every id and leaves the source UNTOUCHED.

source_object.description:
A canonical object — the doc's §6 `objects` row shape, verbatim (all eleven fields).
Content (`content_units`/MathContent) is a SEPARATE aggregate arriving in slice 1;
an object with zero units is valid, which is what makes that addition purely
structural. Named `CanonicalObject` (not `Object`) so the generated TS type never
shadows JavaScript's global `Object`.

id.description: Client-minted UUIDv7 (arch doc §4/§6.3).

type.description:
Durable workspace identity of an object (arch doc §6.0b: `object.type` ≠ `unit.type`).
Slice 1 widens this from the skeleton's lone `note` to the full §6 vocabulary: the
formal family (theorem/lemma/proposition/corollary/conjecture/claim), definition,
proof, example, question — all producible via create — plus `journal_day`, producible
since slice 2b via its §6.5 surface (not the plain typed POST). `source_excerpt`, `trail`,
and `annotation` remain **reserved vocabulary** (valid on read, not yet producible — their
detail tables / surfaces land in later slices, §6.1a/§13a).

title.description: Tri-state (§6.3): `None` = unset, `Some("")` = explicitly empty — never collapsed.

raw_source.description: The rough input, preserved VERBATIM (§2.2). No normalization function exists.

status.description:
Object lifecycle (arch doc §5.2/§6) — the full, doc-stable set lands now even though
the skeleton's create path can only PRODUCE `Draft`; other values become producible
via future status-transition operations.

schema_version.description: Application-level model version (§6.3); migrated by the core's total functions.

revision.description: Optimistic-concurrency token (§6.4): increments on every persisted write.

provenance_id.description: Identity of a provenance row (glue-minted UUIDv7).

source_content.description:
The working content aggregate an op transforms: one object's units plus its concurrency
token. NOT a persisted row — the glue assembles it from `content_units` + `objects.revision`
(§6.0b/§6.4) and writes the resulting `OpOutcome` back.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

items.description:
A content unit — the §6.0b `content_units` row. Content is the authored material;
`content_kind` is a DERIVED SQL column (the `content` tag), so it is NOT a field here.

id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

parent_unit_id.description: THE nesting mechanism: a child names its parent (must be in the same object, §6.1a).

slot.description:
Container-internal part where needed, e.g. `assumption` (case child) /
`justification` (proof_step child); `None` otherwise.

row_relation.description:
The relation this row asserts toward its prior sibling — a `Derivation` step's connective or
an `Equations` row's leading relation; `None` otherwise (the common `Equations` case).

type.description: THE one user-facing math-flow label; `None` = plain content.

status.description:
Per-unit crystallization state (§6.0/§2.4) — distinct from `MathExpression.parse_status`
(surface renderability) and from `ObjectStatus` (object lifecycle).

declared_by.description:
Who declared a unit's STRUCTURE (its type/kind) — a separate fact from content
provenance (§6.0). `ai` is deliberately absent: AI proposals are review_items, never
canonical units; acceptance enters as `user` with AI provenance/derivation (§3.9).

extracted_structure.description: Optional candidate decomposition — declared, never written in slice 1 (§6.0).

kind.description: A value from the small named registry (e.g. `hypothesis_conclusion_decomposition`).

accepted_into.description: The version it materialized into, once accepted (audit trail) — `None` until then.

content.description:
A SINGLE unit's own authored material (§6.0) — never an embedded unit tree (children
are rows: `parent_unit_id` + `position` + `slot`). The internal `kind` tag is the
AUTHORITATIVE discriminator and the source of the derived `content_kind` SQL column.

oneOf.description: Prose + inline formatting + inline math + mentions.

items.description:
An inline element of prose (§6.0): minimal formatting, inline math, or a mention.

**The span contract** — `span` always char-indexes into the prose `text`, but two shapes:
  • An element that carries its **own content field** is a ZERO-WIDTH ATOM (`span = [p, p]`):
    its surface lives in that field, NOT in the prose `text`, so there is a single source of
    truth (§2.2 — no `Reference.text`-vs-`prose[span]` drift). `Math` (content in `expr`) and
    `Reference` (content in `text`) are atoms; the prose `text` holds no characters for them.
  • `Mark` carries no content — it is a REGION `[s, e)` overlaying real prose `text`
    (formatting). It may be non-empty.
Inline `$…$` math is thus a positional atom, not its own unit (else one sentence explodes into
units); promoting it to display math (`$$`) is `ops::toggle_expression_placement`. The
zero-width invariant for atoms is enforced by `validate::validate_inline` (not just convention),
so a future op/editor cannot silently reintroduce width. Rich formatting waits for the editor
(slice 2); MVP `Mark` carries an open-ended `style` tag so import/export round-trips losslessly.

oneOf.description: A formatting region over prose `text` — `span` may be non-empty (the marked range).

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

oneOf.description: Inline math — a zero-width atom (`span = [p, p]`); content lives in `expr`.

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

expr.description:
The one structure for all math, wherever it sits (§6.3a). Identity is STABLE and
presentation-independent; display mode is DERIVED from placement, so there is no mode
field. The `mathmeander` parser/serializer that fills these fields lands in slice 1b;
this is the wire shape it produces.

id.description:
Stable identity, minted in content (UUIDv7), unique workspace-wide — the
copy-mints-fresh / transclude-references rule hangs off this (§6.3a).

surface_text.description: The canonical surface in our grammar (names-canonical `mathmeander`).

original_input.description: Raw keystrokes, preserved verbatim — NEVER overwritten by normalization (§2.2).

parse_status.description:
Whether a surface is usable, per OUR parser (arch doc §6.3a) — defined by `mathmeander`,
not LaTeX. Un-parseable input is never punished: it persists as `invalid` with
`original_input` intact (§2.2).

- `renderable`    — parses cleanly into a fully-formed AST.
- `partially_resolved` — parses, but with one or more recovered error fragments
  (`Expr::Error`); still renderable, with the bad parts shown verbatim.
- `invalid`       — nothing usable parsed (e.g. empty after trimming, or all error).
- `unresolved`    — reserved for "parsed, but has unresolved occurrences/references"
  (set by the core once occurrence resolution runs; the parser never emits it).

occurrences.description:
Semantic refs inside the surface; each extracts to an edge (§6.1b). Always present
(`[]` when none) so the shape is explicit on both sides of the FFI.

items.description:
A semantic reference INSIDE a `MathExpression` (§6.3a). MVP selector is a coarse char
span (Q3); sub-term `Symbol` resolution (with `BoundVar`) is reserved. `target` is
absent while unresolved (the queue carries the unresolved text on the `links` row).

selector.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description: A mention — a zero-width atom (`span = [p, p]`); the surface lives in `text`.

span.description:
A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in
shape to the type the core embedded in slice 1a — relocated here (the surface owns the
char-span model) and re-exported by the core. The artifact `$defs` for it are byte-
identical to 1a's; the artifact HASH did move (the doc-comment-derived `description`
text changed), so do not hand-edit the recorded hash — it is regenerated by codegen.

text.description:
The literal reference surface ("Bolzano–Weierstrass") — kept verbatim; the
resolved edge lives in `links` (§6.1b).

anyOf.description:
Resolution target of a prose `Reference` marker (§6.0): a whole `Object`, or a specific `Unit`
(block) within one. `notation`/`source` arms are reserved (slices 2/3). Absent while unresolved.

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description:
A block within an object — the citation grain for "the definition of an open set" /
"Theorem 3.2". `unit_id` is globally unique (so it survives any future re-homing); `object_id`
is its current home (a resolution hint and the derived Link's `target_object_id`).

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

unit_id.description:
Identity of a content unit (`content_units` row, §6.0b) — what annotations
anchor to, AI candidates target, and edges point at.

link_id.description:
Identity of the `from_content` `Link` this mention derives — CLIENT-minted (like unit
ids; the core mints none), so it is STABLE across content edits and the edge is
reconciled by id, not by position. `None` = not yet stamped (no edge derived).
`save_content` derives/reconciles the edge from this and the `target`.

target_handle_id.description:
Which AUTHORED NAME this mention chose (§6.3b) — the `Handle` whose CURRENT string the
citation displays (rename → the citation updates; identity-not-characters, §3). `None` =
cite by number / the unit's primary name. Display-only: the derived `Link` still targets
the unit, so `save_content` ignores this field.

oneOf.description: A display/block PLACEMENT of one `MathExpression` — not a separate math model.

presentation-independent; display mode is DERIVED from placement, so there is no mode
field. The `mathmeander` parser/serializer that fills these fields lands in slice 1b;
this is the wire shape it produces.

A character-offset span into a surface (`[start, end)`), the wire span. Identical in

object_id.description: Identity of a canonical object (client-minted UUIDv7).

oneOf.description: Items are child rows.
Steps are child rows; each step child carries its relation (slice 1b+).

Co-equal, jointly-asserted relation rows — the "system of equations" `{2x+y=1; x−y=4}`.
Children are `Math`/`Prose` rows (`parent_unit_id` → this container); each row may carry a
`row_relation`. Names the MATHEMATICS, not the layout — column alignment is DERIVED at render
(never stored). Distinct from `Derivation` (sequential) and `CaseSplit` (conditional).
Each case is a child group; `slot` marks the assumption child vs body children.
THE generic container — a typed unit's multi-part body, or a neutral grouping.

A titled section anchor (§B). The title IS this unit's own prose — `text` + `inline`, with the
full prose vocabulary (formatting `Mark`s, inline `Math` atoms, `Reference` mentions), so a
heading can read "§3 Properties of $L^2$". The section's BODY and SUBSECTIONS are this unit's
CHILD rows (`parent_unit_id` → this heading); a subsection is just a child `Heading`. Because the
title lives ON the unit (not in a child), every section unit is 1:1 with one editor block — the
flat-flush invariant — and a section move is a single `reparent_unit` (the subtree follows). LEVEL
is nesting depth (no level field, §B). Distinct from the anonymous `Group`: a `Heading` is titled
and participates in the outline / numbering. The title edits freely through `save_content` (like
`Prose`); becoming/un-becoming a heading is the `toggle_heading` op (a content-KIND change, frozen
from the coarse delta like every other kind transition).
